parameters.query:
SELECT
  u.id,
  {{$('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id}}::bigint AS telegram_chat_id,
  (u.id IS NOT NULL) AS user_found
FROM (
  SELECT id
  FROM users
  WHERE telegram_chat_id = {{$('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id}}
  LIMIT 1
) u
RIGHT JOIN (SELECT 1) dummy ON true;

INSERT INTO users (
  telegram_chat_id,
  first_name,
  last_name,
  username
)
VALUES (
  {{$('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id}},
  '{{$('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.from.first_name?.replace(/'/g, "''") || ""}}',
  '{{$('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.from.last_name?.replace(/'/g, "''") || ""}}',
  '{{$('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.from.username?.replace(/'/g, "''") || ""}}'
)
RETURNING id, telegram_chat_id;

parameters.description:
Set or update the daily digest schedule.
Use this tool when the user wants to enable, create, or change their digest delivery time.
The user provides the desired time (e.g. "9 утра", "21:30") and optionally a timezone.

value.timezone: ={{ $fromAI("timezone", "IANA timezone, e.g. Europe/Moscow. Leave empty for default.") }}

parameters.description:
Get the current digest schedule for the user.
Use this tool when the user asks about their digest settings, schedule status, or when the next digest will be sent.

Disable the daily digest schedule.
Use this tool when the user wants to stop, pause, turn off, or cancel their daily digest.

Change the status of an existing idea.

Use this tool when the user wants to pause, resume, complete, or drop an idea.

Allowed statuses:
- active
- paused
- done
- dropped

Use only for existing ideas. Do not invent idea_id.
If the target idea is unclear, first inspect the user's ideas and identify the correct one.

List all ideas for a specific project.

Use this tool when you need to inspect the ideas inside a project, for example before adding a new idea or when the user refers to existing ideas.

Inputs:
- project_id: the project to list ideas from

Returns:
- list of ideas with ids, titles, and statuses

Create a new research idea inside an existing project.

Use this tool when the user clearly wants to save a specific research idea or hypothesis under a project.

Inputs:
- project_id: the target project id
- title: short name of the idea
- description: optional description of the idea
- keywords: a few technical keywords describing the idea

Do not use this tool if the target project is unclear. In that case, ask a short clarification question first.

List the user's existing research projects.

Use this tool when you need to know which projects the user already has, especially before creating a new idea or when the user refers to an existing project.

Return the available project ids and titles so the correct project can be selected.

Create a new research project for the user.

Use this tool when the user clearly expresses that they want to start or track a new research direction.

A project is a high-level research topic (e.g., "diffusion guidance", "mechanistic interpretability", "VLA models").

Inputs:
- title: short name of the project
- description: optional explanation of the research direction
- keywords: a few technical terms describing the project

Do not use this tool for general discussion or hypothetical ideas unless the user intends to save them.

Search the internal paper corpus using semantic vector retrieval.

Use this tool when the user asks:
- what papers we already have on a topic
- what similar papers exist in the internal corpus
- what is in the corpus about a given method, concept, or research direction

Input:
- query: a short technical search query
- top_k: number of results to return, usually 5

Query rules:
- Pass only substantive technical keywords.
- Omit meta-words such as "recent", "latest", "papers", "research", "new".
- Keep the query short and content-focused.

Search arxiv for papers (text search) or fetch a single paper by arxiv_id (id lookup).
Results are automatically saved to the local corpus with embeddings.

Use text search mode (provide 'query'):
- When the user wants fresh papers on a topic
- When semantic_search_papers returns insufficient results
- The LLM chooses sort_by (relevance or submittedDate), sort_order, and max_results based on intent

Use id lookup mode (provide 'arxiv_id'):
- When the user gives a specific arxiv_id
- Before calling semantic_scholar_lookup for a paper not yet in the corpus

value.query: ={{ $fromAI("query", "arXiv search query using canonical phrases, e.g. 'diffusion guidance OR classifier-free guidance'. Leave empty if looking up a specific arxiv_id.") }}
value.arxiv_id: ={{ $fromAI("arxiv_id", "Exact arxiv_id to fetch (e.g. 2401.12345). Leave empty when doing a text search.") }}

parameters.description:
Update the status of an existing project.

Input:
- project_id: the UUID of the project to update (call list_projects first if unsure)
- new_status: one of "active", "paused", "archived"

Semantics:
- active: user is actively working on this research direction
- paused: temporarily on hold but may resume
- archived: closed / no longer pursued

Use this tool when the user says something like "pause project X", "archive the nerf project", "resume NLP work".
Do not use this tool for ideas — ideas have their own statuses (active/paused/done/dropped) and a separate tool update_idea_status.

parameters.text:
=Привет! Я research-ассистент, готовый помогать с твоими исследованиями.

Что я умею:
📁 Хранить твои исследовательские проекты и идеи
🔍 Искать свежие статьи на arXiv — каждый день автоматически и по твоему запросу
📬 Искать статьи в локальном корпусе после их сохранения из arXiv
💻 Искать код и репозитории на GitHub
💬 Присылать персональный дайджест с оценкой релевантности

Как начать:
1. Скажи "создай проект про [тему]" — например, "создай проект про диффузионные модели"
2. Добавь в него идеи — "добавь идею про classifier-free guidance"
3. Настрой время дайджеста — "присылай дайджест в 9 утра по Москве"

Можно сразу просить находить что-то:
• "найди у меня в корпусе статьи про [метод]"
• "поищи на arXiv свежие работы по [теме]"
• "найди репозитории про диффузионные модели на github"

Говори на естественном языке, я его понимаю. /help — полный список возможностей.

=Что я умею — формулируй запросы как удобно.

📁 Проекты и идеи
• создай проект "Название" с описанием "..."
• список проектов / список идей
• измени статус проекта на активный, "на паузе", или заархивируй его
• измени статус идеи на активную, "на паузе", завершенную или отклоненную
• добавь идею "..." в проект "..."

🔍 Поиск статей
• найди в моём корпусе статьи про X
• поищи на arXiv свежие работы про Y
• посмотри статью 2506.11039

💻 Поиск по GitHub
• найди репозитории про diffusion models
• поищи код по запросу "guidance scale"
• покажи содержимое файла `src/main.py` из репозитория `user/repo`

📬 Дайджест
• настрой дайджест на 9:00 по Москве
• измени время дайджеста на 21:30
• отмени рассылку дайджеста

Меню команд (кнопка / в поле ввода):
/start — начало работы
/help — этот список
/digest — дайджест сегодня
/projects — мои проекты
/ideas — мои идеи
